title: NOC 73112 – Painters and decorators (except interior decorators) | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 73112 professionals are the aesthetic and protective specialists of Canada's building industry. As a TEER 3 occupation, Painters and Decorators apply paint, wallpaper, and other finishes to the interior and exterior surfaces of residential, commercial, and industrial structures. In 2026, as Canada scales its national housing supply and prioritizes the restoration of heritage landmarks, these workers are classified as Essential Finishing Trades. For immigration, this role is a prime target for the 2026 Trade-Specific Express Entry draws and the Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP).

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level3Major Group73 – General tradesSub-Major Group731 – General construction tradesMinor Group7311 – Roofers, glaziers, painters, decorators and floor covering installersNOC Code73112

duties: Blueprint & Specification Reading: Read and interpret architectural specifications to determine the exact quantities and types of materials required for the project.
Surface Preparation: Prepare and clean surfaces using methods such as scraping, sanding, sandblasting, hydro-blasting, and steam-cleaning.
Material Removal: Safely remove old wallpaper, loose paint, and hazardous coatings using appropriate chemical or mechanical strippers.
Structural Repair: Repair cracks and holes in walls using fillers, putty, or plaster; apply sealers to ensure a smooth, durable base.
Color Mixing: Mix and thin paint to obtain the precise color, texture, and consistency required by the client or architectural plans.
Application Techniques: Apply paint, stains, lacquer, enamel, oil, varnish, or fire retardants using brushes, rollers, and advanced spray equipment.
Wallpapering: Measure, cut, and precisely apply wallpaper or decorative fabric to interior walls to ensure seamless transitions.
Safety & Scaffolding: Assemble and erect scaffolding, swing stages, and ladders to safely access high-rise or hard-to-reach areas.
Client Consultation: Advise customers on the selection of color schemes, choice of wall coverings, and the durability of different finishes.
Estimation: Calculate the surface area of projects to provide accurate cost and material estimates for residential and commercial clients.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a three-to-four-year apprenticeship program is the standard path to becoming a journey-level painter.
Experience: Alternatively, a combination of over three years of work experience in the trade and some industry courses may qualify you for trade certification.
Trade Certification:Compulsory in Quebec.Available but voluntary in all other provinces and territories.
Available but voluntary in all other provinces and territories.
Red Seal Endorsement: Highly recommended for all immigrants; it is a major asset for the Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP) and ensures national recognition.
Safety Training: Completion of a Working at Heights or Fall Protection course is mandatory for most construction site work.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, specialized Industrial Maintenance Painters or those working on high-priority infrastructure (like bridges) often earn annual salaries between $65,000 and $88,000 inclusive of overtime.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. With the aging population in Canada, a significant "Retirement Wave" is expected in the finishing trades. Furthermore, as the federal government mandates energy-efficient retrofits for millions of buildings, the demand for painters to apply thermal and protective coatings will increase, providing long-term job security through 2033.
labour_market_demand: The demand for Painters and Decorators in 2026 is Stable to Strong, especially in the Greater Toronto Area (GTA) and Metro Vancouver. While many painters are self-employed (over 50% in some regions), construction firms and maintenance contractors are reporting a shortage of Industrial Painters and Lead-Based Paint Abatement Specialists. According to 2026 labor market reports, the sector is heavily reliant on the "Renovation and Maintenance" market, which remains resilient even during economic fluctuations.
High Self-Employment Potential: This trade has one of the highest rates of entrepreneurship, allowing skilled newcomers to quickly establish their own painting businesses.
Technological Advancement: Gain experience with airless spray systems, electro-static painting, and "Green" VOC-free coatings required for modern sustainable buildings.
Interprovincial Mobility: With a Red Seal endorsement, you can practice your trade in any province or territory without further examination.
Priority Immigration Status: IRCC continues to prioritize skilled trades in 2026, often inviting candidates in the Trade Occupations Category with lower CRS scores.
pathways: Express Entry (Trade Occupations Category): This NOC is a high priority for 2026 category-based selection draws.
Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP): For those with 2 years of experience and a valid job offer or certificate of qualification.
Skilled Trades BC / OINP Skilled Trades: Provincial streams that offer a direct nomination for painters currently working in Canada.
Saskatchewan SINP (Hard-to-Fill Skills): Ideal for painters with a job offer from an approved employer in Saskatchewan.
